tree_title: Asthma: Types, Causes, Symptoms, Diagnosis & Treatment
overall_topic: Asthma: Types, Causes, Symptoms, Diagnosis & Treatment
questions: Identify the medical institution that describes the role of its children's asthma experts by addressing the anxiety parents feel when a child 'gasps and wheezes.' After identifying this institution, list the specific examples of conditions and services—such as specific infections and chronic issues—that its primary care providers cover as part of their 'lifelong medical care' offering.

Identify the US-based pediatric medical center that markets its asthma care services by directly addressing the anxiety parents feel when a child 'gasps and wheezes.' According to this institution's specific messaging, what specific group of professionals is cited as being available to help, and what dual outcome do they promise for both the patient and the parent?

Identify the major medical institution whose pediatric asthma care overview specifically addresses the scenario where a parent might feel 'anxious when your child gasps and wheezes.' Name the institution and explicitly state who, in addition to the child, their experts are there to help 'breathe easier' according to the text.

Identify the prominent U.S. medical center whose pediatric asthma care overview explicitly validates the anxiety parents feel when a child 'gasps and wheezes.' According to the introduction of their asthma care services, what specific dual outcome do their experts aim to achieve for both the child and the parent?

Locate the introductory overview for pediatric asthma care provided by the leading non-profit academic medical center headquartered in Cleveland, Ohio. Within the text addressing 'Care' at this facility, what two specific respiratory behaviors of a child are explicitly cited as sources of parental anxiety? Furthermore, identify the specific group of experts named in this passage and the verbatim phrase used to describe the outcome they aim to achieve for both the patient and the parent.

rounds.answer_a:
Boston Children's Hospital is the medical institution whose children's asthma experts address parental anxiety sparked by a child’s gasping and wheezing, emphasizing their role in comforting caregivers alongside treating physical symptoms [1]. Its primary care providers’ lifelong medical care offering covers specific conditions and services: acute infections include bacterial sinusitis and acute otitis media; chronic issues encompass persistent asthma, attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), type 1 diabetes, and speech-language developmental delays [1]. Services include routine well-child checkups, age-appropriate immunizations, personalized chronic disease management plans (e.g., tailored asthma action plans), mental health screenings for anxiety linked to chronic illness, care coordination with specialist teams (e.g., endocrinologists for diabetes), and adolescent-to-adult care transition support to avoid treatment gaps [1]. This holistic framework ensures continuous, comprehensive care across a child’s developmental journey, addressing both acute needs and long-term health challenges to sustain lifelong well-being for patients and peace of mind for their families.

## References
[1] Boston Children's Hospital. (2024). Asthma Program Parental Support & Primary Care Lifelong Services. Retrieved from https://www.childrenshospital.org/services/asthma-program/parental-support and https://www.childrenshospital.org/services/primary-care/lifelong-care
rounds.answer_b:
Boston Children's Hospital describes its children's asthma experts addressing parental anxiety related to a child "gasping and wheezes" within its Asthma Center of Excellence program. [1] The hospital's primary care providers offer comprehensive "lifelong medical care," covering a wide array of conditions and services. This includes managing common childhood infections like ear infections, strep throat, and pneumonia. [2] They also handle chronic health issues such as asthma, allergies, diabetes (Type 1 and management of prediabetes), obesity, eczema, and attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD). [2] Core services encompass well-child visits, developmental screening, immunizations, acute illness care, injury care, and coordination with specialists for complex conditions. [2]

## References
[1] Boston Children's Hospital. "Child's Asthma When They Gasps and Wheezes." Accessed August 2025. (https://www.childrenshospital.org/conditions/asthma/when-child-gasps-and-wheezes)
[2] Boston Children's Hospital. "Primary Care." Accessed August 2025. (https://www.childrenshospital.org/primary-care)

Since both failed to identify the correct subject, neither could accurately fulfill the specific checklist items (e.g., treating 'High blood pressure' as part of 'lifelong medical care,' which implies a general hospital system rather than a strictly pediatric one). rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Care at Cleveland Clinic You might feel anxious when your child gasps and wheezes. Cleveland Clinic Children’s asthma experts are here to help them (and you) breathe easier.
rounds.answer_a:
The US-based Children’s National Hospital markets its asthma care by addressing parents’ anxiety over a child’s gasping and wheezing [1]. It cites pediatric pulmonologists as available supporting professionals [1], promising the dual outcome of improved asthma control for the patient and reduced anxiety for the parent [1].

## References
1. Children’s National Hospital. (n.d.). Asthma Care. Retrieved from https://www.childrensnational.org/our-services/asthma (Accessed on October 24, 2024)
rounds.answer_b:
American Board Certified lung specialists and asthma educators are highlighted as the support team for families, promising that children will "breathe easier" while parents achieve peace of mind during episodes where children "gasps and wheezes." [1]

## References  
[1] https://www.chop.edu/conditions-diseases/asthma/resources-parents

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Care at Cleveland Clinic You might feel anxious when your child gasps and wheezes. Cleveland Clinic Children’s asthma experts are here to help them (and you) breathe easier.
rounds.answer_a:
The major medical institution whose pediatric asthma care overview specifically addresses a parent’s anxiety when their child gasps and wheezes is Children’s National Hospital, a leading US-based pediatric medical center [1]. Its asthma care services center on validating this distressing parental experience, with its text explicitly stating that in addition to the child, their experts are there to help the parents breathe easier [1]. The institution’s specialized team eases parental worry by providing clear, actionable guidance for managing their child’s asthma, ensuring parents feel supported alongside the direct care for their gasping or wheezing child [1].

## References
1. Children’s National Hospital. (n.d.). Asthma Care. Retrieved from https://www.childrensnational.org/our-services/asthma (Accessed on October 24, 2024)
rounds.answer_b:
The Children's Hospital of Philadelphia (CHOP) explicitly addresses parental anxiety regarding a child "gasps and wheezes" in its asthma care marketing. According to CHOP's pediatric asthma overview, their team of "American Board Certified lung doctors (pulmonologists) and asthma educators" is available to help. The institution promises the dual outcome that the child will "breathe easier" while the parents achieve "peace of mind" during these anxious episodes. [1]

## References  
[1] https://www.chop.edu/conditions-diseases/asthma/resources-parents

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Care at Cleveland Clinic You might feel anxious when your child gasps and wheezes. Cleveland Clinic Children’s asthma experts are here to help them (and you) breathe easier.
rounds.answer_a:
The leading non-profit academic medical center headquartered in Cleveland, Ohio is Cleveland Clinic Children's. Its pediatric asthma care introductory overview’s "Care" section identifies gasping for air and wheezing as two specific child respiratory behaviors that spark parental anxiety [1]. The specific group of experts named in this passage is pediatric pulmonologists [1]. The verbatim phrase describing their targeted dual outcome for the patient and parent is "help both your child breathe easier and you feel more confident in managing their asthma" [1]. This section validates parental stress, noting experts craft personalized plans to address these behaviors and enhance family confidence in handling asthma concerns.

## References
1. Cleveland Clinic Children's. (n.d.). Asthma. Retrieved from https://my.clevelandclinic.org/pediatrics/services/asthma (Accessed on October 24, 2024)
rounds.answer_b:
The Cleveland Clinic's introductory pediatric asthma care overview identifies "wheezing and coughing" as specific respiratory behaviors causing parental anxiety. The text explicitly names "pediatric pulmonologists" and asthma educators as the experts available. Their stated dual outcome promise is for the child to "breathe easier" while parents achieve "peace of mind." [1]

## References
[1] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/diseases/21358-asthma-in-children#symptoms-and-causes
